mainEntity.name: Is Manyata Tech Park Hebbal suitable for registering a company?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — Manyata Tech Park provides commercial office space with registered office facilities. The park's infrastructure and proximity to the airport make it ideal for companies with international operations or FDI.

mainEntity.name: Can you handle FEMA compliance for companies at Manyata Tech Park?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — many companies at Manyata receive FDI or have international transactions. Virtual Auditor provides FEMA valuation, FC-GPR filing, and cross-border compliance from our Bangalore office.

mainEntity.name: What is the cost difference between Hebbal and Koramangala for company registration?

acceptedAnswer.text: Government fees and professional charges are identical. The only variable is office rent for the registered office address — Hebbal/Manyata tends to be 20-30% lower than Koramangala for comparable commercial space.

mainEntity.name: Do I need transfer pricing documentation for my business in Hebbal, Bangalore?

acceptedAnswer.text: If your company has international transactions with Associated Enterprises (foreign parent, subsidiary, or related entity) — yes. TP documentation and Form 3CEB certification are mandatory regardless of the transaction value. Non-compliance: penalty of 2% of international transaction value.

mainEntity.name: How much does transfer pricing documentation cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: TP documentation + Form 3CEB: ₹75,000-₹3,00,000 depending on number and complexity of transactions. TP dispute representation (TPO/DRP/ITAT): ₹1,50,000-₹5,00,000. APA application: ₹3,00,000-₹10,00,000. Virtual Auditor serves businesses across Bangalore.

mainEntity.name: What is the difference between DRP and CIT(A) for TP disputes?

acceptedAnswer.text: DRP: 3-member panel, 9-month timeline, TPO must follow directions, no pre-deposit required. CIT(A): regular appeal, no time limit, pre-deposit may apply. The choice is IRREVERSIBLE — once elected, you cannot switch. Virtual Auditor advises based on case-specific analysis.

mainEntity.name: What methods does Virtual Auditor use for TP benchmarking?

acceptedAnswer.text: All six prescribed methods: CUP, TNMM, CPM, RPM, PSM, and other methods. We use Capitaline, Prowess, and EMIS databases for comparable company identification. TNMM is most commonly applied for IT/ITeS and service transactions.

mainEntity.name: Can the TPO adjust management fees paid to a foreign parent?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — management fee is the most commonly adjusted TP transaction. TPOs argue 'no tangible benefit' to the Indian entity. Defense requires: service agreement, contemporaneous evidence of services rendered, benefit test documentation, and arm's length pricing benchmarked against independent service providers.

mainEntity.name: What is the FEMA-TP dual pricing trap?

acceptedAnswer.text: FEMA floor price (minimum for NR share issuance) may differ from the TP arm's length price. If FEMA floor is higher than ALP: the company pays tax on the FEMA price but the TPO may impute income at the ALP. This conflict requires coordinated structuring of FEMA valuation and TP documentation.

mainEntity.name: What is the Safe Harbour rule for IT/ITeS companies?

acceptedAnswer.text: Safe Harbour allows IT/ITeS companies to declare operating margins of 17-18% without detailed TP documentation — if they meet the conditions. Available for companies with aggregate transactions up to ₹300 crore. CBDT Notification 21/2025 expanded the scope. Virtual Auditor advises on Safe Harbour eligibility.

acceptedAnswer.text: Transfer Pricing Services in Hebbal costs ₹75,000 – ₹5,00,000. Stamp duty: ₹10,000 (BSEC portal — highest among metros). Contact Virtual Auditor at +91-99622 60333 for a specific quote.

mainEntity.name: What documents are needed for transfer pricing services in Hebbal?

acceptedAnswer.text: Requirements vary by service type. Contact Virtual Auditor at +91-99622 60333 for a specific document checklist for transfer pricing services in Hebbal.

mainEntity.name: How long does transfer pricing services take in Bangalore?

acceptedAnswer.text: Timeline varies by complexity. Company registration: 7-10 days. GST registration: 3-7 days. Valuation: 2-3 weeks. Contact Virtual Auditor for specific timelines.

@graph.name: How to Get Transfer Pricing Services in Hebbal, Bangalore

step.name: TP Applicability Assessment
step.text: Determine if your international transactions with Associated Enterprises exceed the threshold for TP documentation. Identify all international transactions: purchase/sale of goods, services rendered/received, management fees, royalty, cost sharing, loans, and guarantees.

step.name: Functional Analysis (FAR)
step.text: Document the Functions performed, Assets employed, and Risks assumed by each party to the international transaction. FAR analysis is the foundation for selecting the Most Appropriate Method and determining the arm's length price.

step.name: Select Most Appropriate Method (MAM)
step.text: Evaluate all six prescribed methods: CUP (Comparable Uncontrolled Price), TNMM (Transactional Net Margin Method), CPM (Cost Plus Method), RPM (Resale Price Method), PSM (Profit Split Method), and any other method. Select MAM with documented rationale.

step.name: Benchmarking Study
step.text: Identify comparable companies using databases (Capitaline, Prowess, EMIS). Apply quantitative and qualitative filters. Compute the arm's length range using the interquartile range of comparable company PLIs (Profit Level Indicators). Verify that the tested party's PLI falls within the arm's length range.

step.name: TP Documentation and Form 3CEB Certification
step.text: Prepare the TP documentation (local file) covering transaction description, FAR analysis, MAM selection, benchmarking, and arm's length price determination. File Form 3CEB (accountant's report) by the ITR due date — certified by a Chartered Accountant.

step.name: Dispute Resolution (if TPO Adjustment)
step.text: If the TPO proposes an adjustment: respond to the TPO's draft order within 30 days. Choose DRP (Dispute Resolution Panel) or CIT(A) — this is an irreversible choice. File appeal before ITAT if DRP/CIT(A) order is adverse. Virtual Auditor represents at all levels.
